title: Cheesy Fingers and Orange Dust Delight
story: Crunch time arrives every March 5 when cheese doodle lovers celebrate their favorite orange snack! People across the country munch on these puffy, cheesy treats that leave telltale orange dust on their fingers. Some folks have cheese doodle eating contests to see who can finish a bag the fastest. Others get creative by crushing them up to use as coating for chicken or mixing them into mac and cheese recipes. Social media fills with photos of orange-stained fingers and creative cheese doodle recipes. Whether you prefer the crunchy kind or the puffy ones, this day gives everyone permission to enjoy these guilty pleasure snacks without any shame!
activity_question: What do people often get on their fingers when eating cheese doodles?
activity_choices: Orange dust
Yellow powder
Red crumbs
Purple glitter
bonus_fact: Some people use crushed cheese doodles as a coating for fried chicken!

title: Multiple Personality Day: Be Everyone You Want!
story: On March 5, people celebrate Multiple Personality Day by exploring all the different sides of themselves! This quirky holiday encourages everyone to embrace their many interests, moods, and talents instead of trying to fit into just one box. Some people dress up as different characters throughout the day, switching from superhero to scientist to artist. Others might try new hobbies they've always wanted to explore, like painting in the morning and playing guitar at night. Social media fills with posts showing people's various passions and personalities. It's a fun reminder that being complex and having multiple interests makes you interesting, not confusing!
activity_question: What do some people do on Multiple Personality Day to show their different sides?
activity_choices: Dress up as different characters throughout the day
Only wear one color all day long
Stay in bed and watch movies
Eat the same meal three times
activity_answer: Dress up as different characters throughout the day
follow_up_question: What are three different sides of your personality that you'd like to celebrate?

title: What's in a Name? Everything!
story: Your name isn't just random letters your parents picked! On March 5, people celebrate Learn What Your Name Means Day by discovering the cool stories behind their names. Kids search online databases, ask family members, or visit the library to uncover whether their name means "brave warrior," "bright star," or something totally unexpected. Many people share their discoveries on social media, and teachers often turn it into fun classroom activities where students present their name meanings to friends.
activity_question: What do people do on Learn What Your Name Means Day?
activity_choices: Research the meaning and history of their names
Change their names to something cooler
Only eat foods that start with their first initial
Write their names backwards all day
activity_answer: Research the meaning and history of their names
follow_up_question: What do you think your name means, and would you choose a different one?

title: Frozen Food Gets Its Cool Day
story: Every March 5, people celebrate National Frozen Food Day by appreciating all the icy treats and meals sitting in their freezers! Families might cook frozen pizza for dinner, grab some frozen yogurt, or stock up on frozen vegetables at the grocery store. Some people share photos of their favorite frozen foods on social media, while others try a new frozen meal they've never tasted before. It's a day to remember how frozen foods make cooking easier and keep our favorite treats fresh all year long!
activity_question: What do people often do on National Frozen Food Day?
activity_choices: Share photos of frozen foods on social media
Build snowmen in their yards
Only eat hot soup all day
Throw away all frozen food
activity_answer: Share photos of frozen foods on social media
bonus_fact: The first frozen foods were sold in grocery stores in 1930!

title: Nutty Clusters of Crunchy Joy
story: Crunchy peanuts covered in sweet chocolate create the perfect treat on National Peanut Cluster Day every March 5! People celebrate by making homemade clusters in their kitchens, mixing roasted peanuts with melted chocolate or caramel. Candy stores showcase their best peanut cluster creations, while families share recipes passed down through generations. Some folks even host peanut cluster taste tests to find the ultimate combination of nuts and sweetness.
activity_question: What do people mix with peanuts to make clusters on this holiday?
activity_choices: Chocolate or caramel
Ice cream and sprinkles
Peanut butter and jelly
Marshmallows and graham crackers
bonus_fact: Peanut clusters were accidentally invented when a candy maker spilled peanuts into melted chocolate!
